name: Product delivery team graph
description: A fuzzy but machine-checkable information architecture connecting product intent, research evidence, delivery reporting, and decisions across two reusable agent loops.

actors.name: Product Owner
actors.description: Owns product intent, priorities, and the final synthesis of research and delivery reports.

selector.description: A trusted product-owner agent accountable for product decisions.

responsibilities: Keep priorities, constraints, and acceptance intent current.
Resolve conflicting signals and publish the decision rationale.

actors.name: Developer team
actors.description: Designs and builds the product, then reports verified progress, risks, and blockers.

selector.description: One or more agents able to design, implement, and verify the selected increment.

responsibilities: Turn the delivery brief into a verified increment.
Surface blockers and decisions needed without waiting for a status meeting.

actors.name: Research council
actors.description: Collects, challenges, and synthesizes decision-relevant evidence.

selector.description: Agents with complementary discovery, skepticism, and synthesis strengths.

responsibilities: Return uncertainty and counterarguments with every recommendation.

knowledge.name: Product intent
knowledge.description: The outcome, audience, constraints, and priority the product should serve; this describes where the current value lives but never embeds the private content itself.

freshness.description: Refresh whenever scope, priority, or a governing constraint changes.

locator.description: Resolve the current product-intent RAC entry through the host at runtime.

knowledge.name: Delivery brief
knowledge.description: The current priority, acceptance intent, and boundaries developers should implement.

knowledge.name: Delivery status
knowledge.description: Progress, verification results, risks, decisions needed, and blockers reported by delivery.

knowledge.name: Research evidence
knowledge.description: Decision-relevant evidence, uncertainty, counterarguments, and source-quality notes.

knowledge.name: Product decision
knowledge.description: The prioritized decision and rationale produced after research and delivery signals converge.

routes.purpose: Translate product intent into a clear delivery brief for the developer team.
routes.success: Developers can state the priority, boundaries, and acceptance intent in their own words.

triggers.description: Send when work is ready or product scope changes materially.

expects.description: Delivery returns progress or a blocker through the declared reporting route.

routes.purpose: Frame the decision question and request evidence before prioritization.
routes.success: The council can distinguish the decision, known constraints, and open uncertainty.

routes.purpose: Return evidence, uncertainty, and counterarguments to the decision steward.
routes.success: The Product Owner can trace the recommendation and see what remains uncertain.

routes.purpose: Report delivery progress, verified outcomes, blockers, risks, and decisions needed.
routes.success: The Product Owner can distinguish verified completion, uncertainty, and the next decision.

cadence.description: Report at verified milestones, blockers, and material scope changes.

loops.description: Research and challenge evidence before a product decision.
Design, build, and review the selected product increment.

convergence.description: Research and delivery signals meet before the next product priority is committed.

policy.description: The Product Owner reconciles evidence, delivery reality, and product intent, then records the rationale.

convergence.trigger: When either loop reaches a decision-ready milestone or reports a material change.
convergence.failureMode: Escalate missing or contradictory evidence instead of silently choosing a priority.
